q: The function and the formation of images in optical instruments are easily understandable with the help of _______ diagrams.
A _______ is an optical instrument that forms and records an image of an object.
In a camera, the _______ focuses the light from the object on the film.
In a camera, the _______ controls the amount of light energy falling on the film.
In a camera, the _______ is a circular opening through which the light passes.
The aperture of a camera can be made wide or narrow by adjusting the _______.
The size or the diameter of the aperture is expressed in terms of the _______.
The f-number of a camera is the ratio of the focal length of the lens to the _______ of the aperture.
When taking a photograph in bright light a _______ f-number must be used.
When photographing in bright light a _______ aperture must be used.
When photographing in dim light a _______ f-number must be used.
When taking a photograph in dim light a _______ aperture must be used.
The shutter controls the _______ the light enters the camera through the lens.
The shutter speed is expressed in terms of the _______ time.
The focusing ring adjusts the distance between the lens and the _______.
The film at the back of the camera is actually a _______ which receives the image.
The film consists of a thin layer of chemical compounds of _______.
A chemical reaction occurs when light energy falls on the _______ of the camera.
The image is formed on the film because of the _______ reaction.
In a _______ camera, the film is replaced by an electronic sensor.
The image sensor in a digital camera is made up of millions of tiny _______ which respond to light.
The human eye consists of _______ organs which are capable of receiving visual images.
The human eye is very similar to a _______ in many aspects.
The _______ is the coloured portion of the eye.
The _______ controls the amount of light energy entering the eye.
The _______ of the eye is in the centre of the iris.
The eyelid is similar to the _______ of the camera.
The front portion of the eye is covered by a membrane called the _______.
The liquid between the cornea and the eyelens is called the _______ humour.
The liquid between the eyelens and the retina is the _______ humour.
The _______ adjusts the size of the pupil in the eye.
The pupil automatically dilates in _______ light.
The pupil automatically contracts in _______ light.
The _______ muscles are attached to the eyelens.
The _______ can vary the focal length of the lens to obtain a sharp image on the retina.
When near objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles _______.
When _______ objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles make the eyelens more convex.
When _______ objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles relax.
The _______ is the light-sensitive area of the cells, at the back of the eye.
The retina consists of rods and _______.
The _______ spot is the most light-sensitive spot on the retina.
The _______ spot is used for viewing very small objects.
The yellow spot consists of _______ exclusively.
The _______ spot of the human eye has no rods or cones.
The _______ spot is where the optic nerve enters the eye.
When an image is formed at the _______ spot, it cannot be seen at all.
The light energy falling on the retina is transmitted to the _______ using the optic nerve.
Farsightedness may be due to an eyeball which is too _______.
Farsightedness can be corrected by the use of spectacles with a _______ lens.
Nearsightedness may be corrected by the use of spectacles with _______ lens.
People with _______ vision can see objects near or far.
For normal vision, the maximum distance of distinct vision is _______.
The least distance of distinct vision is about _______.
When objects at _______ are viewed, the eyes are relaxed.
_______ optics is applied to analyse the formation of an image in microscopes.
A common type of microscope is a _______ microscope.
A compound microscope has at least _______ convex lenses.
In a compound microscope, the lens nearer the object is the _______ lens.
In a compound microscope, the lens through which the final image is viewed is called the _______ lens.
In a compound microscope, the object is _______ if Fo is the focus of the objective.
In a compound microscope, the image formed by the objective becomes the object for the _______ lens.
In a compound microscope, the image formed by the object must be within the focal length of the _______.
In a compound microscope, the final image can be seen clearly when the eye is very close to the _______.
The magnifying power of a compound microscope is the _______ of the magnification of the objective and the magnifying power of the eyepiece.
For a large magnifying power of a compound microscope both the objective and the eyepiece must have _______ focal lengths.
In a compound microscope, the focal length of the objective must be _______ than that of the eyepiece.
In a compound microscope, the power of the objective is _______ than that of the eyepiece.
Compound microscopes have the magnifying power of several _______ times.
As the magnifying power increases, the _______ of the images is reduced.
An electron microscope employs _______ beams in place of light.
An electron microscope uses _______ lenses.
Optical microscopes have a maximum magnifying power of _______.
The magnification of the electron microscope can reach _______.
Electron microscopes deliver a more detailed image compared to _______ microscopes.
_______ microscopes use electrostatic or electromagnetic lenses.
Optical microscopes use _______ or light energy.
In an electron microscope, a beam of accelerated _______ is used as a source of illumination.
SEM and TEM use an electron beam to _______ the specimen and create an image.
In a TEM, the accelerated electron beam is focused by electrostatic or electromagnetic _______ and transmitted through the specimen.
When the electron beam emerges from a specimen, it carries information about the _______ of the specimen.
The information about the structure of the specimen is magnified by the _______ lens of the electron microscope.
When using an electron microscope, the image may be viewed by projecting the magnified electron image onto a _______ viewing screen.
When using an electron microscope, the image can be recorded by a _______ film or by a digital camera.
Electron microscopes are used to investigate the _______ of a wide range of biological and inorganic specimens, metals, and crystals.
